solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def findRepeatedDnaSequences(self, s: str) -> List[str]:
        seen = set()
        repeated = set()
        for i in range(len(s) - 9):
            seq = s[i:i+10]
            if seq in seen:
                repeated.add(seq)
            else:
                seen.add(seq)
        return list(repeated)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses two sets to efficiently track seen and repeated sequences. It iterates through the string once, extracting each 10-letter substring and checking for repetition. The use of sets ensures constant-time lookups and insertions, making the algorithm both time and space efficient.
